title: anyhow -- Flexible Error Handling
description: Provides anyhow::Error, a trait object-based error type for easy error handling in applications. Anyhow lets you use the ? operator with any error type, attach context to errors, and create ad-hoc errors. Designed for applications (not libraries).

commonPatterns.title: Ad-hoc errors with anyhow! macro
Chaining context for better error messages
bail! for early returns
Downcast to the original error type

whenToUse: In applications (binaries, CLIs, services) where you want easy error propagation without defining custom error types for every module. Perfect for prototyping and for main() functions.
alternatives: thiserror (for libraries that need typed errors), eyre (similar API, colored reports with color-eyre), snafu (context selectors, more structured). For libraries, prefer thiserror over anyhow.
